title: Annos influence on Rebuild
wikitext:
[]: Anno's influence on Rebuild             Quote Post by skikes » Fri Jul 23, 2010 2:47 pm What role exactly is Anno playing in the Rebuild franchise? There seems to be a lot of confusion on the matter. From what I can tell he doesn't seem to have had much part in it and simply just handed it to Kazuya Tsurumaki and let him run with it. Thoughts? Clarifications?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Jul 23, 2010 2:48 pm Evangelion 1.0 and 2.0 were written by Hideaki Anno. He was also the chief director.

[]:             Quote Post by Nonoriri » Fri Jul 23, 2010 2:50 pm Chief Director, Screenplay, some Key Animation. Tsurumaki and Masayuki are both co-directors

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Jul 28, 2010 12:57 pm Plus there were interviews with Anno-sama stating that Rebuild was his try to 'correct' EVA's aftereffects in the anime industry and that Rebuild would be Eva 'as it was intended to be'. They assembled the old team as far as possible and you can really feel how Anno has overcome his depression in the mean time...

[]: Re: Anno's influence on Rebuild             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Wed Jul 28, 2010 1:05 pm skikes wrote: From what I can tell he doesn't seem to have had much part in it

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Wed Jul 28, 2010 1:31 pm Kendrix wrote: They assembled the old team as far as possible and you can really feel how Anno has overcome his depression in the mean time...

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Wed Jul 28, 2010 2:48 pm Kendrix wrote: Rebuild would be Eva 'as it was intended to be'

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Jul 28, 2010 5:36 pm Im pretty sure he said that, but I can't seem to find that stupid interview. It was one of the earliest, tough...

[]:             Quote Post by loyal servant » Tue Sep 21, 2010 10:08 am Kendrix wrote: Plus there were interviews with Anno-sama stating that Rebuild was his try to 'correct' EVA's aftereffects in the anime industry and that Rebuild would be Eva 'as it was intended to be'

[]:             Quote Post by Lonecow12 » Tue Sep 21, 2010 11:26 am Yeah I would also like to see where he said "intended to be". You can't say something like that and then not back it up with proof. lol I heard that it was his answer to the current anime industry in the same way the first one was.... oh wait I don't have an article to pack that up.... I'm a hypocrite.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Tue Sep 21, 2010 11:54 am 1) Rebuild is not made by Gainax 2) Anno left Gainax 3) He created a new company called Khara. 4) Khara made Rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Sep 21, 2010 12:14 pm Lonecow12 wrote: Yeah I would also like to see where he said "intended to be". You can't say something like that and then not back it up with proof. lol

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Sep 21, 2010 12:17 pm No, I don't think that's the case (that Anno made NGE "about subtext" for budgetary reasons). There's no grounds for that. Furthermore Nadia out of all Anno's works resemble *least* how he wanted it to be like. Then again as far as I know there's no interview around where Anno said RoE is how NGE was ment to be. He was referring specifically to Ramiel fight in similar quote

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Tue Sep 21, 2010 12:49 pm I thought Khara is like to Gainax as was Manshuukoku was to the Empire of Japan...

[]:             Quote Post by berto » Tue Sep 21, 2010 12:53 pm intended to be might have more to do with animation quality than the direction of the story. they've done things now that they couldn't pull off back in 97.

[]:             Quote Post by Lonecow12 » Tue Sep 21, 2010 1:05 pm berto wrote: intended to be might have more to do with animation quality than the direction of the story. they've done things now that they couldn't pull off back in 97.

[]:             Quote Post by loyal servant » Tue Sep 21, 2010 9:29 pm berto wrote: intended to be might have more to do with animation quality than the direction of the story. they've done things now that they couldn't pull off back in 97.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Sep 22, 2010 3:38 am Lonecow12 wrote: How many scenes are there with Maya or Ritsuko talking with a clipboard over their mouth.

[]:             Quote Post by Lonecow12 » Wed Sep 22, 2010 10:24 am Mr. Tines wrote: >Gender: Male Did you mean "How many scenes are there with Rei or Asuka talking while all you see of them is neck to knee."?

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Sep 22, 2010 11:36 am Anno's influence on Rebuild? Um.... making it. Either way, Rebuild just strikes me as Anno & the original Eva crew playing around & having fun with the universe they created. NGE was made during a period of limited funds, stress, depression, and just absolute drama. Of course that created the wonderful, mulit-faceted universe we're all still enamored with 15 years later. Rebuild is a chance for Anno & crew to play with the elements of this world in a new, larger, and less stressful environment. And I'm loving it. I'd much rather have Rebuild than an expensive and bland Live Action Franchise.
summary:
[]: Anno's influence on Rebuild             Quote Post by skikes » Fri Jul 23, 2010 2:47 pm What role exactly is Anno playing in the Rebuild franchise? There seems to be a lot of confusion on the matter. From what I can tell he doesn't seem to have had much part in it and simply just handed it to Kazuya Tsurumaki and let him run with it. Thoughts? Clarifications?

[]:             Quote P
